description:
You are a Range Safety Officer at NASA Wallops Flight Facility preparing for a commercial resupply launch. Configure GPredict to monitor crewed space stations along your launch corridor.

1. Create the Launch Corridor Ground Stations:
   - Wallops (Primary Launch Site): Latitude 37.8330 N, Longitude 75.4833 W, Altitude 4m
   - Bermuda_Downrange (Downrange Telemetry Site): Latitude 32.3430 N, Longitude 64.6540 W, Altitude 10m
   - Antigua_Downrange (Downrange Telemetry Site): Latitude 17.0130 N, Longitude 61.7610 W, Altitude 30m

2. Create the COLA Tracking Module:
   - Create a new module named COLA_Monitoring.
   - Assign this module to use the Wallops ground station for its predictions (do not use the global default).
   - Add these crewed space station components: ISS (ZARYA) (NORAD 25544), ISS (POISK) (NORAD 36086), ISS (NAUKA) (NORAD 49044), and CSS (TIANHE) (NORAD 48274).

3. Configure the Module Layout for Situational Awareness:
   - Modify the layout of the COLA_Monitoring module so it displays exactly 3 views: a Map view, a Polar view (radar), and a List view.

4. Set Global Operational Time:
   - Configure GPredict to display UTC time instead of local time.

Login: username 'ga', password 'password123'. GPredict is already open.
